content.text:
All 13 tests pass now.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
This code path (`report_argument_not_assignable`) exclusively handles call-argument sites — the sibling assignment-site diagnostics live in `assignability_diagnostics.rs`. The widening rule wasn't "widen when suggesting a call" (TS2560), it was "widen when at a call site" (both TS2559 and TS2560), while assignment-site NoCommonProperties diagnostics always preserve literals. The bug conflated "which diagnostic code we picked" with "which site we're at" — those happened to correlate in the one test that existed before, masking the real distinguishing condition.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

The root cause: the `NoCommonProperties` branch only called `widen_weak_type_callable_source_display` when tsc would also suggest "Did you mean to call it?" (TS2560), leaving the plain TS2559 call-argument variant with an unwidened literal (`{ a: 1 }` instead of `{ a: number }`). Since this whole function only handles call-argument sites (assignment-site widening is handled separately and correctly preserves literals), the fix makes the widening unconditional here — matching tsc's actual rule (call-site widens, assignment-site preserves) rather than the code-path-specific rule that happened to work for the one prior test case.

result:
All 13 tests pass now.

`★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────`
This code path (`report_argument_not_assignable`) exclusively handles call-argument sites — the sibling assignment-site diagnostics live in `assignability_diagnostics.rs`. The widening rule wasn't "widen when suggesting a call" (TS2560), it was "widen when at a call site" (both TS2559 and TS2560), while assignment-site NoCommonProperties diagnostics always preserve literals. The bug conflated "which diagnostic code we picked" with "which site we're at" — those happened to correlate in the one test that existed before, masking the real distinguishing condition.
`─────────────────────────────────────────────────`

The root cause: the `NoCommonProperties` branch only called `widen_weak_type_callable_source_display` when tsc would also suggest "Did you mean to call it?" (TS2560), leaving the plain TS2559 call-argument variant with an unwidened literal (`{ a: 1 }` instead of `{ a: number }`). Since this whole function only handles call-argument sites (assignment-site widening is handled separately and correctly preserves literals), the fix makes the widening unconditional here — matching tsc's actual rule (call-site widens, assignment-site preserves) rather than the code-path-specific rule that happened to work for the one prior test case.
